samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. The design supports both signed and unsigned division operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst">The reset signal to initialize or reset the module.</input>
        <input id="sign">1-bit indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">1-bit indicates that a valid operation request is present.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">1-bit indicates that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit the output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">State IDLE = 2'd0;</state>
        <state id="DIVIDE">State DIVIDE = 2'd1;</state>
        <state id="DONE">State DONE = 2'd2;</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="res_valid_reg" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="start_cnt" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="cnt" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="neg_divisor" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="dividend_temp" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="divisor_temp" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="result_reg" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="SR" init_value="0"/>
        <always_comb>
            if(rst == 1'b1)begin
                res_valid_reg <= 1'b0;
            end else if(cnt == 8 && res_valid_reg != 1'b1)begin
                res_valid_reg <= 1'b1;
            end else if(res_valid_reg == 1'b1 && res_ready == 1'b1)begin
                res_valid_reg <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                res_valid_reg <= res_valid_reg;
            end
        end
        always @(posedge clk) begin
            if(opn_valid == 1'b1 && res_valid_reg == 1'b0)begin
                dividend_temp <= dividend;
                divisor_temp <= divisor;
            end else begin
                dividend_temp <= dividend_temp;
                divisor_temp <= divisor_temp;
            end
        end
        always @(posedge clk) begin
            if(opn_valid == 1'b1 && res_valid_reg == 1'b0)begin
                if(sign == 1'b1)begin
                    SR <= {dividend[7], dividend} << 1;
                end else begin
                    SR <= dividend << 1;
                end
            end else if(start_cnt == 1'b1)begin
                if(SR[7:0] >= divisor_temp)begin
                    SR <= {SR[7:0] - divisor_temp, SR[15:8]};
                end else begin
                    SR <= {SR[7:0], SR[15:8]};
                end
                if(cnt == 8)begin
                    cnt <= 1'b0;
                    start_cnt <= 1'b0;
                end else begin
                    cnt <= cnt + 1'b1;
                end
            end else begin
                cnt <= cnt;
                start_cnt <= start_cnt;
            end
        end
        always @(posedge clk) begin
            if(opn_valid == 1'b1 && res_valid_reg == 1'b0)begin
                if(divisor[7] == 1'b1)begin
                    neg_divisor <= ~divisor + 1;
                end else begin
                    neg_divisor <= divisor;
                end
            end else begin
                neg_divisor <= neg_divisor;
            end
        end
    </endlogic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Division operation starts when opn_valid is high and res_valid is low
        if (opn_valid == 1'b1 && res_valid == 1'b0) begin
            // Save the inputs dividend and divisor
            dividend_temp <= dividend;
            divisor_temp <= divisor;
            
            // Initialize the shift register SR with the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit
            if (sign == 1'b1) begin
                SR <= {dividend[7], dividend} << 1;
            end else begin
                SR <= dividend << 1;
            end
            
            // Set NEG_DIVISOR to the negated absolute value of the divisor
            if (divisor[7] == 1'b1) begin
                neg_divisor <= ~divisor + 1;
            end else begin
                neg_divisor <= divisor;
            end
            
            // Set the counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process
            cnt <= 1;
            start_cnt <= 1;
        end
        
        // If start_cnt is high, perform the division process
        if (start_cnt == 1'b1) begin
            // If the counter cnt reaches 8 (most significant bit of cnt is set), the division is complete
            if (cnt[7] == 1'b1) begin
                // Clear the counter cnt and start_cnt
                cnt <= 0;
                start_cnt <= 0;
                
                // Update the shift register SR with the final remainder and quotient
                SR[15:8] <= SR[15:8];
                SR[7:0] <= SR[7:0];
            end else begin
                // Increment the counter cnt
                cnt <= cnt + 1;
                
                // Perform the subtraction operation
                if (SR[7:0] >= divisor_temp) begin
                    // If the result of the subtraction is non-negative, subtract the divisor from the current value of SR
                    SR <= {SR[7:0] - divisor_temp, SR[15:8]};
                end else begin
                    // If the result of the subtraction is negative, keep the current value of SR
                    SR <= {SR[7:0], SR[15:8]};
                end
            end
        end
        // Manage the validity of the result
        if (rst == 1'b1) begin
            // Reset the result_valid signal to 0
            res_valid <= 0;
        end else if (cnt == 8) begin
            // Set the result_valid signal to 1 when the counter cnt reaches 8
            res_valid <= 1;
        end else if ((res_valid & res_ready) == 1'b1) begin
            // Clear the result_valid signal when it is consumed
            res_valid <= 0;
        end else begin
            // Maintain the current value of result_valid otherwise
            res_valid <= res_valid;
        end
    </implementation>
</module>
